Respondent Farmers Insurance Exchange does not dispute that Ms. Starikova has an underinsured motorist claim and is entitled under the policy to arbitration. The parties shall raise issues regarding the scheduling and scope of the arbitration with the selected arbitrator in the first instance. rulings.Case Title: DAVID RAWLINGS ET AL VS. TALK FREE, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 30, 2019, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANT TALK FREE, INC. WHICH WILL DO BUSINESS IN MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. (part 1 of 2 of tentative ruling) Judge Schulman discloses that he previously practiced law in a firm with Mr. Skaggs. That will not affect his ability to fairly and impartially decide this matter. Defendants' motion for summary adjudication is granted and denied in part. The motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1-11. The motion is procedurally infirm under CCP § 437c(f)(1) because it does not completely dispose of the breach of fiduciary duty causes of action. For example, issue 5 relates to the allegation at paragraph 38 of the Amended Complaint that there was a breach of fiduciary duty because Mrs. Scheele hired consultants to take over Talk Free, the consultants refused Mr. Rawlings' access to the books, the consultants refused to pay vendors, and they even lied to a Telco partner. The three adduced facts with regard to this issue fail to show that this theory of liability "cannot be established" within the meaning of CCP 437c(p)(2). Unlike Lilienthal & Fowler v. Sup.Ct. (1993) 12 Cal.App.4th 1848, defendant does not seek to adjudicate separate and distinct wrongful acts that are combined in the same cause of action. The breaches of fiduciary duty pertain to a related train of events and a single injury. (See Crouse v. Brobeck, Phleger & Harrison (1998) 67 Cal.App.4th 1509, 1526, fn.2 ["Nothing in the summary judgment statute allows a single cause of action . . . to be separated into component acts with separate statute of limitations running on each discrete act"]; see also Bagley v. TRW, Inc. (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 1092, 1094, fn.2 [questioning whether Lilienthal properly construed section 437c(f)(1)].) Defendants' reliance on the language of section 437c(f)(1) that permits a court to grant summary adjudication as to an issue of duty is misplaced, as that language refers to the existence and scope of duty as a matter of law, not the factual issue of alleged breaches of duty. (Paramount Petroleum Corp. v. Superior Court (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 226, 243 ["there is no statutory basis for summary adjudication on the issue of breach."].) The motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issue 12 as conceded. If plaintiffs wish to add a new theory of fraud liability, then they must file a motion to amend in compliance with CRC 3.1324. The motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 13. Plaintiffs may be entitled to an accounting based upon their breach of fiduciary duty claims. The court rejects defendants' argument that the court should grant summary adjudication as to Talk Free on this claim. While it is true that a predicate claim is necessary for an accounting under Janis v. Cal. State Lottery Comm. (1998) 68 Cal.App.4th 824, 833, defendants' notice of motion does not raise the issue as to Talk Free. Issue 13 only refers to The Scheele Trust. The motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 14-18 as conceded. The motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 19. Defendants fail to maintain their initial burden of production. Plaintiff alleges the existence of a March 1, 2013 loan agreement and defendants' facts refer to an ostensibly unrelated promissory note that is dated February 19, 2013. Defendants' argument that plaintiff admitted that the February 19, 2013 note was the only basis for this claim misstates plaintiff's deposition testimony. (Rawlings Depo., 467:21-468:16.) (end of part 1 of 2 )=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ADRIENNE HOGAN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Ruling Denying Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 30, 2019, 2018, Line 11, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion For Reconsideration Of Ruling Denying Motion For Summary Judgment. (specially set at 9:00 am before Judge Kahn) The hearing will be at 9am, not 9:30am. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for reconsideration of the January 9, 2019 order denying the City's motion for summary judgment is denied. There is a triable dispute whether plaintiff Adrienne Hogan was a "shift operator" as that term is used in 22 CCR 63770. Ms. Hogan says she wasn't and the City says she was. While Ms. Hogan's statements that she was not a shift operator were not explicitly made in a declaration under penalty of perjury, that omission may be cured by administering the oath to her at the hearing and is not a basis for a non-legally trained self-represented litigant to lose her claims. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Ms. Hogan is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD HANSEN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, MUNI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ; Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 30, 2019, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. (part 1 of 2 of tentative ruling) Defendant the City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment is granted. As a general rule, a plaintiff must present a public entity with a timely written claim for damages before filing suit against it. (J.M. v. Huntington Beach Union High School Dist. (2017) 2 Cal.5th 648, 652; Gov't Code §§ 905, 911.2, 915.) Timely claim presentation is not merely a procedural requirement, but rather, a condition precedent to a plaintiff's maintaining an action against a defendant, and thus, an element of the plaintiff's cause of action. (K.J. v. Arcadia Unified School Dist. (2009) 172 Cal.App.4th 1229, 1238.) A claim shall contain, among other things, the amount claimed by the claimant as of the date of presentation of the claim, including the estimated amount of any prospective injury, damage, or loss. (Gov't Code § 910(f).) It shall be presented in writing and signed by the claimant. (Id. § 910.2.) The claim shall be presented by delivering or mailing it to the clerk, secretary or auditor of the local public entity. (Id. § 915(a).) If the claim, as here, relates to a cause of action for personal injury, it shall be presented not later than six months after accrual of the cause of action. (Id. § 911.2.) When a claim is not presented within that period of time, a written application may be made to the public entity for leave to present the claim within a reasonable time not to exceed one year after the accrual of the cause of action and shall state the reason for the delay in presenting the claim. (Id. § 911.4.) It is undisputed plaintiff did not present a written claim to the City within the meaning of the Government Claims Act within six months after the March 9, 2016 accident. Plaintiff's failure to present a timely claim bars his lawsuit. (Id. § 945.4; DiCampli-Mintz v. County of Santa Clara (2012) 55 Cal.4th 983, 990 [" 'Thus, under these statutes, failure to timely present a claim for money or damages to a public entity bars a plaintiff from filing a lawsuit against that entity.' "].) Plaintiff's telephonic report of his accident to the Muni customer service line did not constitute substantial compliance with the Act because it did not satisfy the express delivery provisions language of the statute, even if it was sufficient to place the City on notice of the general nature (if not the amount) of his claim. (DiCampli-Mintz, 55 Cal.4th at 991; see also Garber v. City of Clovis (E.D. Cal. 2010) 698 F.Supp.2d 1204, 1214-16 [doctrine of substantial compliance did not apply where there was a complete failure to serve any responsible officer or entity]; California Restaurant Management Systems v. City of San Diego (2011) 195 Cal.App.4th 1581, 1591 [" '[i]t is well-settled that claims statutes must be satisfied even in the face of the public entity's actual knowledge of the circumstances surrounding the claim. Such knowledge-standing alone-constitutes neither substantial compliance nor basis for estoppel.' "].) The City did not have any duty under Government Code section 910.8 to notify Plaintiff of the insufficiency of that telephonic report because he failed in the first instance to present a claim as defined in the Act. The City has carried its burden to show that Plaintiff's telephonic report did not constitute a claim that was actually received by the appropriate City representatives, and Plaintiff has not presented evidence sufficient to give rise to a triable issue of fact on that issue. Accordingly, the City is entitled to summary judgment. (Id. at 991-997; Life v. County of Los Angeles (1991) 227 Cal.App.3d 894, 899-901.) (end of part 1 of 2) =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD HANSEN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, MUNI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Summary Judgment. (For Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 30, 2019, 2018, Line 4, Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Summary Judgment. (part 2 of 2) (For Tentative Ruling Only) There is no triable issue of fact regarding the City's asserted estoppel to assert the claims statutes. The Muni representatives made no affirmative representation to Mr. Hansen that he need not file a written claim in compliance with the Act, or that his telephonic complaint would be sufficient to satisfy the Act's requirements. In Santos v. Los Angeles Unified School Dist. (2017) 17 Cal.App.5th 1065, in contrast, plaintiff informed a government representative that she wanted to file a government claim for damages arising out of an automobile accident involving the L.A. School Police Department, and was advised to download a specific claim form from the LASPD website and mail the completed form to a specific address, without ever mentioning that LASPD was a part of the L.A. Unified School District and that plaintiff would have to file a separate claim with that distinct entity. (Id. at 1076-1077.) Even if Mr. Hansen reasonably could have relied upon the Muni customer service representative's statement that his complaint would be processed or the email stating that his passenger service report had been disseminated to several departments and superintendents of several divisions for review as an implied representation that he need not file a written claim, his attorney could not have done so. It is undisputed that plaintiff filed the complaint in this action on August 31, 2016, and that the City filed its answer raising the affirmative defense of Plaintiff's failure to comply with the claims statute well within the one-year period for Plaintiff to file an application for leave to present a late claim. At both times, he was represented by counsel, yet he did not file such an application, and has offered no explanation for his failure to do so. "[Plaintiff] fails to establish an injustice. He simply failed to comply with the claims statutes, missing an easily ascertainable deadline that has been in place for over 50 years. [Citation.] If oversight of such plain rules justified equitable relief, the structure of the Government Claims Act would be substantially undermined, and its provisions for timely notice to public entities subverted." (J.M. v. Huntington Beach Union High School Dist., 2 Cal.5th at 658.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for CCSF is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. (end of part 2 of 2) =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: R & G EXECUTIVE LOUNGE, INC. VS. HENRY HUNG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 30, 2019, 2018, Line 12, Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Cross-defendants Frank Wong, Emily Wong, and Karen Liu's Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings is denied as to the breach of loyalty cause of action. The cross-complaint alleges conduct "inimical" to R&G's interests within the meaning of Stokes v. Dole Nut Co. (1995) 41 Cal.App.4th 285, 295. (See also Huong Que, Inc. v. Luu (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 400; Rest. 3rd of Agency, §8.05.) The motion is granted with 20 days leave to amend as to the breach of contract cause of action to allege with more particularity whether the alleged contract was oral or written and the terms of the contract that were allegedly breached. The motion is denied as to the breach of implied covenant cause of action. The contract sufficiently alleges a contractual relationship based on the employer/employee relationship, and cross-defendants' "unauthorized" acceptance of excessive compensation sufficiently alleges a breach of an implied covenant that thwarts R&G's benefit of the bargain. (Cross-Complaint, 14.) The motion is denied as to the conversion cause of action. Paragraph 38 of the cross-complaint alleges an identifiable sum to support the claim. (See SP Investment Fund I LLC v. Cattell (2017) 18 Cal.App.5th 898, 907.) The motion is denied as to the money had and received cause of action. (See Stratton v. Hanning (1956) 139 Cal.App.2d 723, 727.) The motion is denied as to the concealment cause of action. The cross-complaint sufficiently alleges active concealment, which does not require a fiduciary duty, and less particularity in the allegations is acceptable based upon the very nature of a concealment claim. (See Heliotis v. Schuman (1986) 181 Cal.App.3d 646, 651; Tenet Healthsystem Desert, Inc. v. Blue Cross of Cal. (2016) 245 Cal.App.4th 821, 838.) The motion is denied as to the conspiracy to breach fiduciary duty cause of action. "When the nonfiduciary is an agent or employee of the fiduciary, the nonfiduciary is entitled to the benefit of the "agent's immunity rule" (and thus not liable on a conspiracy theory) unless the nonfiduciary was acting for its own benefit." (Everest Investors 8 v. Whitehall RealEstate Ltd. P'ship XI (2002) 100 Cal.App.4th 1102, 1104.) The court rejects cross-defendants' statute of limitations arguments because the cross-complaint alleges facts showing that cross-complainants only recently learned of the malfeasance after the preliminary injunction hearing. (Cross-Complaint, 14; Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 808.) The court rejects cross-defendants' voluntary payment and Labor Code § 221 defenses. Cross-defendants raise factual issues and "unauthorized" payments do not support the defenses. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for cross-defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: R & G EXECUTIVE LOUNGE, INC. VS. HENRY HUNG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Strike Paragraphs 8, 18, And 20 Of The Cross-Complaint Of Henry Hung And Joe K Ling
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 30, 2019, 2018, Line 12, CROSS DEFENDANT ANGEL MEI LIU's Motion To Strike Paragraphs 8, 18, And 20 Of The Cross-Complaint Of Henry Hung And Joe K Ling. Cross-defendant Angel Mei Liu's motion to strike paragraphs 8, 18, and 20 is granted without leave to amend. All references to Ms. Liu's alleged status as a "mistress" are stricken. The cross-complaint must plead ultimate-not evidentiary-facts. Ms. Liu's alleged status is not necessary to support the claims. This ruling is without prejudice to cross-complainants raising the issue regarding Ms. Liu's status on an evidentiary motion or at trial if her alleged status is relevant and meets Evidence Code § 352. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MINOO PARSAZADEH VS. HAMPTON HEALTH, LTD. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defefendant John Fullertons Attendance And Testimony At Deposition And Production Of Documents, And Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JANUARY 30, 2019, LINE 3 PLAINTIFF MINOO PARSAZADEH'S Motion To Compel Defefendant John Fullertons Attendance And Testimony At Deposition And Production Of Documents, And Monetary Sanction Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = PCC

rulings.Case Title: ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC. VS. BRADLEY J. BARKER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order, And Turnover Order
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, JANUARY 30, 2019, LINE 5 PLAINTIFF ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC.'s Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order, And Turnover Order Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff Align Technology, Inc's unopposed Motion is GRANTED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = PCC

rulings.Case Title: ANDREY KUKUSHKIN VS. MED THRIVE COOPERATIVE, INC. DBA MEDITHRIVE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 30, 2019, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANT MED THRIVE COOPERATIVE, INC. DBA MEDITHRIVE, INC., MISHA BREYBURG, SASHA PLOTITSA's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants Med Thrive Cooperative, Inc., Misha Breyburg, and Sasha Plotitsa's demurrer to the second, third and fourth causes of action for breach of fiduciary duty, waste, and unjust enrichment of the second amended complaint is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action and overruled as to the remaining causes of action. The second amended complaint sufficiently pleads that the individual defendants breached their fiduciary duty to nominal defendant Venture Rebel and engaged in waste of its corporate assets by self-dealing and depletion of Venture's funds with the purpose of benefiting Med Thrive. Those allegations are sufficient to support those causes of action, independent of the terms of the Management Services Agreement or Plaintiff's knowledge of that agreement. Plaintiff's discovery responses regarding that agreement are not properly considered by the Court on demurrer. The Second Amended Complaint is a derivative complaint that does not allege any breach of the Stock Purchase Agreement or the Investor Rights Agreement. Plaintiff Andrey Kukushkin is given 20 days leave to amend the pleading and allege facts, if he can do so in good faith, supporting an alternative theory of recovery based on unjust enrichment. (Klein v. Chevron U.S.A., Inc. (2012) 202 Cal.App.4th 1342, 1389-1390 ["a plaintiff may plead inconsistent claims that allege both the existence of an enforceable agreement and the absence of an enforceable agreement.].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Kukushkin is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JON J DEL BINO JR VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 30, 2019, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to the complaint is sustained without leave to amend. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for CCSF is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: AVIS BUDGET GROUP VS. GD TOWING ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 30, 2019, 2018, Line 10, Plaintiff'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction. Plaintiff's motion for preliminary injunction is granted. No opposition filed. The court will execute the proposed order, but will remove references to the DMV and its director (formerly Jean Shiomoto, now acting director Bill Davidson). There is no evidence that those parties have been served with this action or this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number.=(302/EPS)
